name: a retrospective 'I left Acme in March' should close the open employment interval, not conflict
description: SPEC (docs/ANALYSIS.md 'state ... The old fact stays true about its interval' + SCD-2): note A opens an OPEN employer interval (Tomas worksFor Acme since 2023, no end, value_json {state:current}). Note B arriving now ('Tomas left Acme back in March 2026', value_json {state:ended, end:2026-03}) supplies the END of that SAME relationship — a valid_to backfill on the still-open current state, with the same object entity (Acme) and same valid_from (2023). The spec-correct outcome is ONE Acme fact whose interval is now closed (it stays true about its interval) with NO fact_conflict review — nobody disputes Tomas worked at Acme; we merely learned when it ended. GAP: supersession.decide has no 'close interval in place' path. value_json now differs so values_equal is False; with equal valid_from the candidate (later reported_at) wins the >= test and decide() SUPERSEDES the open Acme row, chaining a second Acme row and filing a fact_conflict. So a same-relationship interval close is mishandled as a contested value change, producing two Acme employer rows and a spurious conflict. XFAIL until decide() recognizes an end-date backfill on the same object/valid_from as an in-place interval close rather than a supersession.
xfail: no interval-close path: closing an open interval ('left Acme in March') shares object entity Acme and valid_from 2023 with the open row, but because value_json differs decide() treats it as a contested newest-wins supersession — it chains a second Acme employer row and files a fact_conflict instead of closing the one interval in place. Failing line: "expected 1 facts matching {'entity': 'Tomas', 'predicate': 'employer', 'kind': 'relationship', 'value_contains': 'Acme'}, found 2" (and the spurious fact_conflict review).

steps.body: Tomas has worked at Acme since 2023.
I should note Tomas actually left Acme back in March.

facts.statement: Tomas worked for Acme from 2023 until March 2026.
